title: Concept Bottleneck Models
tagline: Make the model predict human-named concepts first, then read off the label — and intervene if it's wrong
summary: A two-stage classifier that you can debug. The image is mapped to a vector of named concepts ("is dark", "has-stripes", "is-warm") and a small head reads those concepts. Toggle a concept on or off at inference and the prediction changes — the closest thing DL has to causal interpretability.
highlights: Live two-stage model on a 4-class synthetic shape problem; toggle concepts and watch label predictions flip.
Concept-leakage demonstration: when the bottleneck has spare capacity, the head learns to bypass concepts.
Lab-aligned: thermal-respiration concepts (chest / nostril / breath-rate) → apnea label; satellite concepts → kiln class.
